title: Unredacted contract 3640 between City of Seattle and Axon Enterprise

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

An unredacted, complete copy of City of Seattle Supplier Contract #000000000003640 (and all associated change orders) between the City of Seattle and Axon Enterprise, Inc., originally dated on or about November 5, 2024.

To clarify, this request specifically seeks the full, unredacted content of the following sections which were redacted in previous disclosures:
 - Attachment C – Pricing
 - Attachment D – Evidence.com Terms of Use
 - Section 1.26 of Attachment A, Statement of Work (SOW)
 - All Service Level Agreements (SLA) or supplemental terms incorporated by reference into [that] agreement."

Please note:
 - Pricing is Public Post-Award: Under Washington law, pricing for "goods and services" purchased with public funds is not a trade secret under RCW 42.56.270 once a contract has been awarded and executed. The public has a paramount interest in knowing the financial terms of surveillance technology procurement.
 - Terms of Use are Not Exempt: Terms of Use (Attachment D) are not proprietary business methods; they are the legal rules governing the Agency’s use of public data. Under City of Sedro-Woolley v. Rodriguez (2025), data "paid for by" and "generated for the benefit of" the City is a public record regardless of storage location or vendor-imposed terms.
 - Prohibition on Contractual Confidentiality: An agency cannot "contract away" its statutory obligations under the PRA. Any clause in the Axon contract promising confidentiality for non-exempt information is void as a matter of public policy.
 - Narrow Construction of Exemptions: Per RCW 42.56.030, the PRA must be liberally construed in favor of disclosure and exemptions must be interpreted narrowly. The City bears the burden of proving that every redacted sentence—including "Terms of Use"—meets a specific statutory exemption.
 - Constructive Possession: Under the Nissen and O'Neill frameworks, the City maintains "constructive possession" of all terms and data within the Axon/Evidence.com ecosystem. Redacting the terms that define this possession (the SOW and Terms of Use) is a violation of the "fullest assistance" mandate in RCW 42.56.100.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Phil Mocek
